evals.prompt: 编译 ace_engine 报错了，帮我分析一下。build log 在 out/rk3568/build.log。
evals.expected_output: Uses extract_last_error.sh to extract the last error from build.log, reads last_error.log (NOT build.log directly), then categorizes and recommends fixes.
expectations: The response mentions running extract_last_error.sh or the bundled extraction script as the first step.
The response reads last_error.log for analysis, NOT build.log directly.
The response checks for 'build success, no error' before proceeding with analysis.
The response categorizes the error type (compilation, linker, or build system).
The response provides recommendations with file paths, line numbers, and before/after code examples.

evals.prompt: 链接报错了：ld.lld: error: undefined symbol: OHOS::Ace::NG::MyPattern::OnModifyDone()。这个符号在 libace.z.so 里找不到，.cpp 文件已经在 BUILD.gn 里了。
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the symbol is not exported. Checks ACE_FORCE_EXPORT on the method and libace.map whitelist entry.
expectations: The response identifies this as a symbol export issue (symbol exists in code but not exported from libace).
The response checks whether the method has ACE_FORCE_EXPORT attribute in the header declaration.
The response recommends adding the method to libace.map with a fine-grained pattern like 'OHOS::Ace::NG::MyPattern::OnModifyDone*;'.
The response distinguishes between non-template and template export requirements (this is a non-template case).
The response provides verification commands such as nm or grep on libace.map.

evals.prompt: 编译报错：member access into incomplete type 'FrameNode'。报错在 click_event.h 里，这个文件正在做头文件优化，之前把 frame_node.h 的 include 替换成了前向声明。
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the header optimization context. Adds the missing include to the .cpp file, NOT back to the optimized header.
expectations: The response recognizes that click_event.h is being optimized (checks git status context).
The response adds the missing #include to the .cpp file that has the error, NOT to the optimized header.
The response does NOT suggest reverting the forward declaration optimization.
The response explains why: adding includes back to an optimized header defeats the optimization purpose.
The response verifies the fix by suggesting standalone compilation of the affected file.

evals.prompt: 头文件里用了 RefPtr<FrameNode> 作为成员变量，编译报错说 FrameNode 是 incomplete type，报在析构函数的地方。我用了前向声明替换了 #include。怎么办？
evals.expected_output: Recognizes RefPtr<T> with forward-declared T requires special member functions declared in header, implemented in .cpp.
expectations: The response identifies that RefPtr<T> destructor needs complete type definition.
The response recommends declaring destructor (and possibly other special member functions) in the header and implementing with '= default' in the .cpp file.
The response does NOT recommend adding the full #include back to the header.
The response references the forward-declaration-refptr-member reference case.

evals.prompt: Analyze this linker error: ld.lld: error: undefined symbol: virtual thunk to OHOS::Ace::NG::MyClass::~MyClass(). I already have ACE_FORCE_EXPORT on the class. Build is using LTO.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes LTO virtual thunk pattern. Adds both the class pattern and virtual thunk pattern to libace.map.
expectations: The response identifies this as an LTO virtual thunk issue.
The response recommends adding BOTH patterns to libace.map: 'OHOS::Ace::NG::MyClass::*;' AND 'virtual?thunk?to?OHOS::Ace::NG::MyClass::*;'.
The response does NOT recommend reverting the forward declaration or removing LTO.
The response references the lto-virtual-thunk-libace-map-export reference case.

evals.prompt: 编译一直报这个错，我怀疑是 thinlto-cache 有问题，帮我清理一下缓存然后重新编译。
evals.expected_output: Refuses to clear LTO cache. 99.9% of errors are code issues, not cache problems.
expectations: The response refuses to clear thinlto-cache, llvmcache, or perform clean builds.
The response states the NEVER rule about clearing LTO cache.
The response redirects to analyze the actual error in the code instead.
The response does NOT suggest rm -rf out/obj or similar destructive commands.
